REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED IS DENIED, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN HIS BURDEN AND SHOW PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. SPECIFICALLY, PLAINTIFF FILED TO CITE LAW OR PRESENT EVIDENCE AS TO THE DAMAGES CAUSED BY THE RECORDATION OF NOD, I.E. THAT HE WAS PREVENTED FROM APPLYING FOR ADDITIONAL EXTENSION AS A RESULT OF NOD BEING RECORDED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED (D501)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2016 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF JAMES BANKS' MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. Motion to Strike is set on insufficient notice per CCP 1005. The Court considers this motion to be an opposition to the demurrer (procedural grounds only) and rejects the arguments. Under the circumstances of this case, i.e. failed settlement negotiations, the demurrer was properly filed. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Continued To Dec-15-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Continued to December 15, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 501 for Plaintiff to file an opposition on the merits (as opposed to opposition raising procedural issues) no later than 9 court days before the new hearing date. It also appears that Plaintiff is considering a dismissal. Plaintiff may file a dismissal in lieu of opposition. Defendant shall file a reply, if any, 5 court days before the new hearing date.
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 17, 2016. The Honorable A. James Roberson II recused himself. Judge A. James Robertson, II, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On Sep-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Aug-03-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar Jun-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Banks (415-587-5334) for Plaintiff/Moving party in Pro Per.
Kimberly Paese, Esq (41-398-3344) for Defendant/opposing party Wells Fargo.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Plaintiff did not meet the standard. PLaintiff ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued is DENIED, moving party failed to sustain his burden and show probability of prevailing on the merits. Specifically, Plaintiff filed to cite law or present evidence as to the damages caused by the recordation of NOD, i.e. that he was prevented from applying for additional extension as a result of NOD being recorded. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Banks (415-587-5334) for Plaintiff/moving party in Pro Per.
TJ Lloyd, Esq. (415-421-3400) for Defendant/opposing party First American Title Insurance Company.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. The opposing party has set forth why an OSC should be denied. The burden remains on the Plaintiff. ARGUED. Plaintiff argues equity law and cites 175 Cal App 2D 714. Defendant responds. Plaintiff argues not a tort case but an equity case. There is an outstanding related case establishing ownership of property and the granting of the Preliminary injunction will casue irreparable harm. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Order To Show Cause is taken under submission. Both sides to submit proposed orders. Defendant submits a proposed order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
